candidates.title: Windows Firewall
candidates.summaryDraft: Windows Firewall controls inbound and outbound network traffic based on configured rules and profiles.
factsDraft: Firewall profiles commonly include domain, private, and public networks.
Rules can allow or block traffic based on app, port, protocol, or direction.
examTipsDraft: Know when to check firewall rules during application connectivity troubleshooting.

evidence.text: Windows Firewall allows you to configure rules for different network profiles.
Inbound and outbound rules can permit or block application traffic.

candidates.title: Command Prompt
candidates.summaryDraft: Command Prompt is a Windows command-line interface used to run administrative, file, and network commands.
factsDraft: Command Prompt can run tools like ipconfig, ping, and netstat.
Some commands require elevated administrator permissions.
examTipsDraft: Differentiate Command Prompt from PowerShell on scenario questions.

evidence.text: From the command prompt, we can run utilities such as ipconfig.

possibleDuplicates.title: ipconfig
possibleDuplicates.reason: Transcript evidence references ipconfig, but this candidate is broader.
